name: Tributyrin
summary: Butyrate prodrug used in preclinical microbiome-metabolite experiments to restore butyrate-linked intestinal histone acylation and healthspan signals.
mechanism_summary: Delivers butyrate to restore short-chain-fatty-acid signaling and histone acylation marks in model systems where mitochondrial dysfunction and dysbiosis deplete butyrate-linked metabolites.

open_questions: Whether butyrate prodrugs can reproduce the mouse disease-model signal in normal aging or human populations
Whether histone acylation recovery is causal for healthspan or lifespan effects
How dosing, delivery site, and tolerability shape chronic translation
